clusters.headline: Vape flavour restrictions proposed to protect children
clusters.summary: Government is consulting on plans to restrict vape companies from using enticing flavour descriptions that may attract children into experimenting. The proposed measures aim to reduce youth vaping by making product names and flavours less appealing to minors.

stories.title: Vapes to have less enticing names and flavours to protect children

clusters.headline: RAC urges drivers to compare fuel prices
clusters.summary: The RAC advises motorists to use price comparison tools to save money at the pump, as many are missing out on potential savings from an 11p fuel price gap between different retailers.

stories.title: Drivers urged to shop around amid 11p fuel gap

clusters.headline: Meta faces backlash over AI image generation from Instagram
clusters.summary: Meta allows users to create AI images from public Instagram profile pictures, prompting privacy campaigners to warn of potential misuse and calling the feature a 'recipe for disaster' despite an opt-out option for users.

stories.title: Outcry as Meta lets users make AI images from public Instagram profile pics

clusters.headline: Screen time impact on young children examined
clusters.summary: A report explores how increased screen time is affecting the development and wellbeing of younger children, raising concerns about potential long-term impacts on their health and social development.

stories.title: Tech Life

clusters.headline: NHS maternal care faces criticism
clusters.summary: Many fear the NHS will continue to fail mothers and babies without a cultural shift, as families remain failed by the current system. BBC's Michael Buchanan argues meaningful changes are needed to improve outcomes for pregnant women and newborns.

stories.title: Many fear the NHS will continue to fail mothers and babies unless there's a cultural shift

clusters.headline: US business owners sell to employees in retirement
clusters.summary: As US company owners reach retirement age, more are choosing to sell their businesses directly to their employees rather than external buyers, reflecting a growing trend in business succession planning and employee ownership models.

stories.title: Why I sold my business to my staff

clusters.headline: £5 coffee reflects global economic factors
clusters.summary: City centre coffee prices reaching £5 illustrate broader economic trends including tariffs, climate impacts, Gen Z cultural preferences, and strategic pricing by coffee farmers navigating global markets.

stories.title: The £5 coffee that tells a story of global economic turmoil

clusters.headline: Celebrities embrace metallic bikinis in summer trend
clusters.summary: Celebrities are increasingly wearing metallic bikinis, with the shiny swimwear trend gaining popularity this summer. The reflective fabric is drawing attention at beaches and poolside locations.

stories.title: Metallic Bikinis For The Win, Shiny Swimwear to Catch Your Eye

clusters.headline: UK festivals see wellness event surge
clusters.summary: Organisers report increased demand for wellness events such as yoga and running clubs at UK festivals. This trend reflects growing public interest in health-focused activities during large gatherings.

stories.title: Why wellness is booming at festivals in the UK

clusters.headline: Ofcom targets scam ads in tech
clusters.summary: Ofcom proposes stricter regulations for big tech companies to address scam ads, citing that over half of UK adults have encountered fraudulent online advertisements.

stories.title: Big tech must deal with scam ads under Ofcom proposals

clusters.headline: Met investigates donation to Jenrick's campaign
clusters.summary: The Metropolitan Police is investigating a donation to Reform UK leader Jack Dorneen's leadership campaign. Dorneen denies the allegations, calling them 'entirely false'.

stories.title: Met investigates donation to Jenrick's Tory leadership campaign

clusters.headline: Starmer receives gun from Turkish president
clusters.summary: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer was gifted a gun and ammunition by Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdoğan during a NATO event. The weapon remains in Turkey with British officials.

stories.title: Starmer gifted gun and ammunition by Turkish president at Nato

clusters.summary: Analysis of the UK's defense investment plan reveals efforts to meet NATO spending targets. The plan includes increased military funding but faces scrutiny over its effectiveness.

Frank Gardner: Key points from government's defence spending plan

clusters.headline: Burnham's devolution plan and economic impact
clusters.summary: Andy Burnham's devolution proposal outlines a alternative approach to UK governance. Critics question whether it constitutes a full economic plan or merely a political vision.

clusters.headline: UK chancellor race begins
clusters.summary: The UK's chancellor position is vacant following the prime minister's resignation. Multiple candidates are vying for the role, marking the start of a political leadership contest.

stories.title: Who could be the UK's next chancellor?

clusters.headline: Brexit's economic impact after a decade
clusters.summary: A decade after Brexit, economists assess its long-term economic effects. While some predicted significant damage, the actual impact appears more nuanced with mixed outcomes.

stories.title: Ten years on, Brexit's economic impact is becoming clearer

clusters.headline: Vaccines saved lives but trust remains issue
clusters.summary: The UK's COVID-19 vaccine rollout saved hundreds of thousands of lives, but public trust in vaccines remains a challenge due to ongoing hesitancy.

stories.title: Vaccines a huge success, but public trust must be earned - key findings from Covid report

clusters.headline: UK Covid inquiry concludes on vaccine roll-out
clusters.summary: The fourth report from the UK's COVID-19 inquiry praises the vaccine roll-out as an 'extraordinary feat' while highlighting areas for improvement in pandemic response.

stories.title: What is the UK Covid inquiry and how does it work?

clusters.headline: Special educational needs support hits record high
clusters.summary: The number of children receiving special educational needs support has reached a new record high, with the largest yearly increase in education, health and care plans (EHCPs) since their introduction. The rise reflects growing demand for specialist educational provisions across the UK.

stories.title: Number of children getting special educational needs support hits another record high

clusters.headline: UK political discontent raises governability questions
clusters.summary: Discontent with the political class has reached unprecedented levels, prompting questions about whether the UK has become ungovernable and whether the role of prime minister has become more challenging than in previous eras.

stories.title: Is it harder than ever to be prime minister?
